title: derris laxiflora
intro: Derris laxiflora, also known as Derris, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia and Australia, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain a compound called rotenone, a natural insecticide and antiseptic. Derris laxiflora is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against infections. In herbal medicine, Derris laxiflora is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
